note: Mathlib/Analysis/SumIntegralComparisons.lean:215 — theorem summable_of_integrableOn_Ioi_zero (anti : AntitoneOn f (Ici 0)) (integrable : IntegrableOn f (Ioi 0)) (nonneg : ...) : Summable (fun n : ℕ ↦ f n); docstring literally '**Integral test**: a nonnegative antitone function is summable if it is integrable.'
verify_note: Decl confirmed in declaration-data.json cache and live mathlib4 checkout at cited line; own docstring says '**Integral test**', and the file's module docstring explicitly names this theorem (paired with tsum_le_integral) as the integral test for antitone functions, distinct from the separately-labeled converse lemma; Wikidata Q1155313 label/description ('integral test for convergence' / test for infinite series of monotonous terms) matches.
